name: Max Morath
description: American ragtime pianist, composer, actor and author (1926–2023)
abstract: Max Edward Morath (October 1, 1926 – June 19, 2023) was an American ragtime pianist, composer, actor, and author. He was best known for his piano playing and is referred to as "Mr.

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: The Stardust Road: A Hoagy Carmichael Jubilee

works.t: The Real American Folk Song is a Rag
Stop! Look! Listen!: I Love a Piano

about_quotes.speaker: Leslie Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: Max Morath play out there, who has become an extremely close friend, a dear, dear man.
about_quotes.doc_title: Leslie Johnson interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Vince Giordano
about_quotes.evidence: And even watching T.V. shows like Joe Franklin, who was a fellow here in New York who is still around, and Max Morath. These would also be people who said yes this is old stuff but it’s classic stuff, it’s great stuff.
about_quotes.doc_title: Vince Giordano interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 11, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Armand Hug
about_quotes.evidence: And then you have this Max Morath who has been on educational TV who really plays a real bang-up ragtime piano.
